title: Fix handling of non-latin credentials in HTTPDigestAuth
body:
This patch fixes handling of non-Latin credentials in HTTP Digest Authentication.

Summary of changes:
- src/requests/auth.py
  - _basic_auth_str: switch encoding from latin1 to utf-8 when converting username and password to bytes. This ensures non-Latin credentials are encoded correctly for Basic authentication header generation.
  - HTTPDigestAuth.build_digest_header: adjust header construction to safely handle non-Latin characters by normalizing username/password to text (decode if bytes) and relying on utf-8 encoding when hashing and assembling the Digest header. This ensures the header string is formed correctly for non-Latin inputs.
- tests/test_requests.py
  - Add tests to verify non-Latin credentials are handled correctly for HTTPDigestAuth, ensuring a successful digest-auth flow with non-Latin usernames/passwords.

Rationale:
- The existing implementation encoded username and password with latin1, which breaks non-Latin credentials and causes authentication failures for internationalized inputs (as reported in issue #6102).
- By using utf-8 for encoding and ensuring the Digest header formatting respects utf-8 text, non-Latin credentials will be treated correctly across both encoding and header construction.

Impact:
- Backward-compatible for ASCII, but fixes correctness for non-Latin credentials.
- Tests now cover non-Latin credentials for Digest authentication to prevent regressions.

step_plan.summary: Fix handling of non-Latin credentials in HTTP Digest Auth by switching encoding to UTF-8 for Basic auth and normalizing/UTF-8 encoding in digest header construction; add tests to cover non-Latin credentials.

steps.reason: Ensure non-Latin credentials are correctly encoded for Basic authentication by using UTF-8 instead of Latin-1, preventing encoding-related failures for international characters.
steps.side_effects: May affect tests relying on Latin-1 encoding semantics; ensure ASCII behavior preserved; downstream code using Basic auth string remains compatible; tests may need updates to cover non-Latin inputs.

steps.operation: Update HTTPDigestAuth.build_digest_header to normalize credentials and use UTF-8 for hashing and header construction
steps.reason: Non-Latin credentials must be handled safely; normalization to text and UTF-8 hashing ensures correct digest for international characters; aligns with _basic_auth_str changes.
steps.side_effects: Digest header formatting changes; potential minor impact on tests; will require new tests for non-Latin credentials; ensure ASCII tests pass.
